title: Chrysoma pinifolia
intro: Chrysoma pinifolia, also known as Pineleaf Chrysanthemum or Chrysanthemum pinifolium, is a type of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Chrysoma pinifolia include flavonoids, sesquiterpenes, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often prepared into teas, infusions, or decoctions, as well as being used in the form of granules or capsules, to promote relaxation, reduce inflammation, and support immune function.
taxonomy: Chrysoma pinifolia is classified within the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification places it among other flowering plants with similar characteristics. The genus Chrysoma is a group of flowering plants native to the Americas, and Chrysoma pinifolia is one of its species. Further, Chrysoma pinifolia is also known as the pine daisy, likely due to its unique appearance. Its classification as a member of the Asteraceae family reflects its botanical relationships with other plants in this family.
